rendering_standard: SVG inline, viewBox 500x350 minimum, aria-label required, answer NEVER encoded in visual, all labeled diagrams include alt text

visual_generators.description: Nested set diagram showing Real Numbers ⊃ Rational ⊃ Integer ⊃ Whole ⊃ Natural; Irrational as separate region inside Real. A given number is highlighted — Mathlete identifies which rings it belongs to.

svg_params.irrational_region: separate oval inside Real, outside Rational

visual_generators.downstream_usage: Classification questions for MF.FB.3.1–3.5 and Grade 8 NS
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Target number is shown but its ring membership is NOT annotated

visual_generators.description: Horizontal number line from −20 to 20 with integer tick marks; target point marked as filled circle; distance arrow from 0 to point shown as double-headed arrow
visual_generators.downstream_usage: MF.ANT.1.1 absolute value; MF.ANT.1.2–1.3 addition/subtraction of integers; MF.AP.5.1 additive inverse
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Distance label NOT shown — Mathlete computes |n|

visual_generators.description: Number line showing addition of integers as sequential arrows: first arrow from 0 to first operand, second arrow from that point the length of the second operand
visual_generators.downstream_usage: MF.ANT.1.2 conceptual model only — NOT used for computation questions
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Endpoint (sum) shown as open circle with '?' label — Mathlete determines the value

visual_generators.description: Number line from 0 to 2 divided into equal segments matching denominator; target fraction marked with open circle — Mathlete identifies the fraction
visual_generators.downstream_usage: MF.FDP.1.1 fraction identification; MF.FDP.2.x conceptual support
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Fraction tick points NOT labeled — Mathlete reads position

visual_generators.description: Two side-by-side rectangles of equal size; one partitioned into n parts with k shaded, the other into more parts with corresponding shading to show equivalence
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: One rectangle has '?' shading — Mathlete finds equivalent fraction

visual_generators.description: Number line showing two consecutive perfect squares with their roots labeled; a point for the target irrational is marked between them

svg_params.target_sqrt: marked between n and n+1, label = √radicand

visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Decimal approximation NOT shown — Mathlete estimates

visual_generators.description: Three triangle types (acute, right, obtuse) with base and perpendicular height labeled. For obtuse: height drawn outside the triangle with dashed extension.

svg_params.height_indicator: small square at foot of altitude

visual_generators.format_hint_rule: CRITICAL: height is always dashed perpendicular, NOT the slant side. Formula NOT shown.

visual_generators.description: Two adjacent angles inside a right angle corner (90° box marker); one labeled numerically, one labeled '?'
Two angles on a straight line (180°); one labeled, one labeled '?'; straight line indicator shown
Two intersecting lines forming 4 angles; one angle labeled numerically, three others labeled 1, 2, 3 — Mathlete identifies value of named angle
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Relationship type (vertical/adjacent) NOT labeled — Mathlete identifies

visual_generators.description: Right triangle with right angle marked; legs labeled a, b with numeric values; hypotenuse labeled c = ?
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Formula (a² + b² = c²) NOT shown on diagram. One side labeled '?'

visual_generators.description: Contextual right triangle illustration — e.g. ladder against a wall with 'wall height', 'ground distance', 'ladder length = ?' labels
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Context labels are descriptive nouns — not variable names

visual_generators.description: Two overlapping circles labeled A and B inside a universal set rectangle; elements placed in correct regions (A only, B only, intersection, outside both)

svg_params.element_display: letters or numbers in correct regions

visual_generators.downstream_usage: MF.SETS.2.1–2.3, MF.SETS.4.2; Grade 7 SP compound events
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: For operation questions, regions are shown with elements but operation result NOT highlighted — Mathlete identifies

visual_generators.description: Two-set or three-set Venn diagram with no elements; shows region shading to represent a named operation — Mathlete identifies which operation is represented

svg_params.shaded_region: randomized (union / intersection / complement / A only / etc.)

visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Operation name NOT labeled on diagram — Mathlete writes which operation produces the shading

visual_generators.description: Two-set Venn diagram with numeric counts in each region (not element lists); one region labeled '?'
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Formula NOT shown. One region is '?' — Mathlete uses n(A ∪ B) = n(A) + n(B) − n(A ∩ B)

visual_generators.description: Horizontal box-and-whisker plot on a number line; Q1 and Q3 walls unlabeled — Mathlete reads position to find IQR

svg_params.label_policy: only min and max labeled; Q1 and Q3 positions shown by box edges

visual_generators.format_hint_rule: IQR value NOT shown. Box edges are visual — Mathlete reads axis and computes

visual_generators.description: Expression tree diagram showing operator precedence visually — the expression is broken into a tree with evaluation order shown as numbered nodes (1 = evaluate first)

svg_params.tree_nodes: operators as nodes; operands as leaves

visual_generators.usage: Conceptual aid for MF.ANT.3.1 static questions only — NOT a generator visual
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Evaluation numbers shown — this is a TEACHING visual only, paired with B-group static questions

visual_generators.description: Rectangle partitioned into two sub-rectangles; total width = (b + c), height = a; each sub-rectangle labeled with its area
visual_generators.usage: Conceptual model for MF.AP.3.1 — illustrates why a(b+c) = ab + ac geometrically
visual_generators.format_hint_rule: Total expression a(b+c) NOT written on diagram. Mathlete writes the expanded form
